mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS > 100k and UNRATE < 0.5pp decline in 1 month
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS/UNRATE signal fails to produce positive returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect's t-statistic < 1.96.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates stronger real economic activity, which translates to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The INDPRO signal generates negative returns in > 40% of triggered instances, or the 5-day return correlation with INDPRO < 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in housing starts (HOUST) during a monetary tightening cycle signals reduced consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The HOUST/FEDFUNDS interaction fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the 60-day return correlation with the signal < -0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2% erodes real returns, prompting central bank rate hikes that depress equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The CPIAUCSL signal generates positive returns in > 50% of instances, or the 20-day return correlation with CPIAUCSL > 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with rising GDP (GDPC1) indicates robust demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through earnings momentum.
failure_condition: The RSAFS/GDPC1 interaction fails to produce positive returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the 60-day return correlation with the signal < 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 5% signals tight labor markets, increasing wage pressures that reduce corporate margins and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The UNRATE signal generates positive returns in > 50% of instances, or the 5-day return correlation with UNRATE > 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Falling industrial production (INDPRO) during a monetary easing cycle signals weak supply-side dynamics, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The INDPRO/FEDFUNDS interaction fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the 20-day return correlation with the signal < -0.15.
